articles.title: Britain’s elite Commandos to get new drones and high-speed boats in £500m defence blitz

Over £500million will be committed to the elite force under the Defence Investment Plan to get the country war-fighting ready. The UK’s frontline Commando Force is capable of deploying anywhere in the world at...

articles.title: SwarmUI does what Midjourney costs $20 a month for, and it runs on my own hardware
Joy Malignant is a photobashed, dice-based RPG where every choice you make affects how your faceless body looks
articles.description: The thing about Citizen Sleeper is that I wish there was more of it. Not literally, necessarily, I'd never want that world to be exhausted of its setting, and my imagination is stronger than any threequel or otherwise. Still, I'd like to play more of it because its inventive approach to digitising tabletop RPG design still feels fresh now. Thankfully, more and more games inspired by its structure are popping up, like Joy Malignant, another dice-based RPG where every choice you make affects how people see you. Read more

articles.title: I ignored storage discipline in Proxmox until a restore actually mattered
articles.description: GPU passthrough gets the attention, but Proxmox only feels reliable when storage, backups, and restores are organized properly.

articles.title: AI and the politics of resistance
articles.description: It is difficult these days to attend a conference, browse social media, or sit through a university meeting without hearing the same refrain: artificial intelligence (AI) is here, and we had better learn to adapt. There is a sense of urgency in the air. Schools are being urged to redesign their curricula. Workers are told [...]... Keep on reading: AI and the politics of resistance

articles.title: What Is a Crypto Card? How It Works, Types, Benefits, and the MEXC Card Explained
articles.description: Crypto cards connect your crypto balance to everyday spending by converting digital assets into fiat at checkout. This guide explains how crypto debit, credit, and prepaid cards work, their fees and trade-offs, and breaks down the MEXC Card—a Visa crypto debit card funded by USDT. MEXC offers direct spending, up to 10% cashback in USDT, 7% APR on balances, and no annual fee.

articles.title: Vivo X500e leak points to new design, Snapdragon 8-series chipset and Zeiss-tuned camera
articles.description: A new leak has surfaced with early details of a Vivo smartphone expected to launch as the Vivo X500e. The device is tipped to feature a refreshed rear design, a 6.59-inch display, and a Snapdragon 8-series chipset.

articles.title: Flextail Blast Air compact air blower for cleaning with Qi charging launches
articles.description: Flextail has launched a Kickstarter campaign for the Blast Air, and it has already been successful. That should allow the compressed-air blower to enter production, complete with a few interesting features.

articles.title: Top 5 Privacy Coins by Market Cap —What to Use?
articles.description: Privacy in crypto isn't one-size-fits-all. Explore the biggest privacy coins, how they work, and what makes each network different. Which one should you use?

articles.title: Why Local LLMs Suddenly Slow Down at Long Context
articles.description: Your local LLM runs fine until the context fills up past a certain point - then generation speed can drop by ~50%. The cause is the KV cache spilling out of VRAM into slower shared memory. On Windows it happens silently, with no out-of-memory error to warn you.

articles.title: AI Exposes the Quality of Your Thinking
articles.description: AI doesn't improve your thinking, it just reveals its quality. Clear thinkers use it to accelerate their work, while unfocused thinkers get polished nonsense. The real danger is letting AI take over your judgment, which is the one thing it can't automate. To stay sharp, use AI as a sparring partner to challenge your ideas, not as a replacement for having them.

articles.title: I tested Cursor’s top alternatives after Elon Musk’s SpaceX agreed to buy it, and one clearly stood out
AI is creating America's next underclass
articles.description: Jensen Huang warns that AI is changing the rules of everyday survival and creating a two-tier caste system, with those who can command AI and those who cannot, and those who refuse to adapt will be left behind.

articles.title: 90 Blog Posts To Learn About Ethical Hacking
articles.description: Let's learn about Ethical Hacking via these 90 free blog posts. They are ordered by HackerNoon reader engagement data. Visit the Learn Repo or LearnRepo.com to find the most read blog posts about any technology. Ethical hacking is the practice of identifying vulnerabilities in computer systems and networks to improve security, performed with the owner's permission. It's vital for proactively protecting digital assets against malicious attacks by simulating real-world threats. 1. 3 Flipper Zero Hacks to Wow Your Friends (and How They Work) It is theoretically possible to use these Flipper Zero hacks for nefarious purposes, including the tricks in this article. But you know...don’t do that. 2. Man In The Middle Attack Using Bettercap Framework Man-in-the-middle attack (MITM) is an attack where the attacker secretly relays and possibly alters the communications between two devices who believe that they are directly communicating with each other. In order to perform man in the middle attack, we need to be in the same network as our victim because we have to fool these two devices. Now lets initiate the attack by running our tools which is bettercap. To run bettercap we can simply open up terminal and type bettercap -iface [your network interface which connected to the network]. To know which network interface is used we can simply type ifconfig and here is what it shows us. 5. 5 Top Programming Languages Every Ethical Hacker Needs To Learn The 21st century is a century of digital citizens as technology has become the most important aspect of our lives. Having the knowledge of programming has become more of a necessity than an option and similar to the growing craze of coding, Ethical hacking has also become extremely popular these days. Everyone wants to become a hacker today especially the youth, but the truth is that hacking and becoming a hacker is not as easy as it is shown in numerous sci-fi movies, in-fact it takes years of practice and programming to become an Ethical hacking expert. There are several Programming languages used for Ethical hacking/ Whitehat hacking that are used extensively. 6. How to Hack Someone's Phone Remotely There are many Hackers in this world who may be currently watching your activities daily and will try to snatch your personal information from you. 7. How to Map Your Home Networks Using NMAP Here's to learning basics of networking and an awesome tool called NMAP 8. He joined us and blessed us for our bright academic records, a very Indian ritual on meeting elderly personalities! 88. How NOT to Secure Web Payment Systems How to get a Covid test for free | Bugs in covid payment systems are everywhere. This is the story of how I found one of them 89. Understanding the background of the Hospital Bed Rental Industry Healthcare organizations that follow HIPAA guidelines must be explicitly aware of security threats. Ethical hacking needs a more critical role in the framework. 90. Ode to 2600 Magazine When I was a kid hacking around BBS's and telenet in the late 1980's, 2600 was the ultimate source, goal, and aspirational destination for anyone that wanted to be heard that was doing something different with computers. Thank you for checking out the 90 most read blog posts about Ethical Hacking on HackerNoon . Visit the /Learn Repo to find the most read blog posts about any technology.
